_comment: Source: NASEMSO National Model EMS Clinical Guidelines v3.0 (March 2022). Toxins and Environmental: Calcium Channel Blocker Poisoning/Overdose. NASEMSO national provisional base.
level_display: All Levels — Interventions Scoped Per Provider Level
protocol_reference: NASEMSO National Model EMS Clinical Guidelines v3.0 — Toxins and Environmental: Calcium Channel Blocker Poisoning/Overdose

sections.title: Patient Care Goals
sections.reference: NASEMSO National Model EMS Clinical Guidelines v3.0 — Toxins and Environmental: Calcium Channel Blocker Poisoning/Overdose
points: Reduce GI absorption of oral agents with some form of binding agent (activated charcoal) especially for extended release
Early airway protection is required as patients may have rapid mental status deterioration
Assure adequate ventilation, oxygenation, and correction of hypoperfusion

sections.title: Patient Presentation
points: Patients who have may have taken/been administered calcium channel blockers a. Calcium channel blocker examples: i. Amlodipine (Norvasc')
ii. Diltiazem (Cardizem', Tiazac')
iii. Felodipine
iv. lsradipine
v. Nicardipine
vi. Nifedipine (Adalat CC', Afeditab CR', Procardia')
vii. Nisoldipine (Sular')
viii. Verapamil (Calan', Verelan')
EXCLUSION CRITERIA:
None noted

sections.title: EMR Interventions
sections.reference: NASEMSO v3.0 — EMR level (Emergency Medical Responder)
points: Scene safety; activate ALS/HAZMAT as needed
Oxygen via NRM; BVM for respiratory failure
CPR/AED for pulseless arrest
Position supine; prevent further absorption (do not induce vomiting)

sections.title: EMT Interventions
sections.reference: NASEMSO v3.0 — EMT level (Emergency Medical Technician)
points: 12-lead ECG: bradycardia, AV block, widened QRS, heart failure common
IV access maintenance (non-medicated)
Monitor closely — CCB overdose can be rapidly lethal; early ALS essential

sections.title: AEMT Interventions
points: IV/IO access initiation; IV fluid bolus 250-500 mL for hypotension
Atropine 0.5-1 mg IV for bradycardia (often ineffective in CCB OD)
Calcium chloride 1 g IV/IO for bradycardia/hypotension (blocks excess CCB effect)
Glucagon 1-3 mg IV for refractory bradycardia/hypotension
Waveform capnography; blood glucose monitoring

sections.title: Paramedic Interventions
points: Calcium chloride 1-2 g IV over 2-5 min; repeat q 10-20 min (monitor ionized calcium)
High-dose insulin euglycemia (HDIE): regular insulin 1 unit/kg IV bolus + 50% dextrose; infusion 0.5 unit/kg/hour + dextrose infusion
Glucagon 3-5 mg IV bolus, then 2-5 mg/hour infusion
Lipid emulsion therapy (intralipid 20%) 1.5 mL/kg IV for refractory toxicity (per medical direction)
Transcutaneous pacing for high-degree AV block
Vasopressors (norepinephrine, epinephrine) for refractory hypotension
12-lead ECG interpretation: identify AV blocks, QRS widening
RSI: succinylcholine may be used; rocuronium 1.2 mg/kg preferred

sections.title: Patient Safety Considerations
points: Transcutaneous pacing may not always capture nor correct hypotension when capture is successful.

sections.title: Educational Pearls
points: While most calcium channel blockers cause bradycardia, dihydropyridine class calcium channel blockers (e.g., nifedipine, amlodipine) can cause a reflex tachycardia (torsade de pointes) early in the ingestion. The patient can become bradycardic as the intoxication worsens

The avoidance of administering calcium chloride or calcium gluconate to a patient on cardiac glycosides (e.g., digoxin) as this may precipitate toxicity and associate fatal arrhythmias is felt to be a historical belief and not supported
Glucagon has a side effect of increased vomiting at these doses and ondansetron prophylaxis should be considered
A single pill can kill a toddler. It is very important that a careful assessment of medications the toddler could have access to is done by EMS and suspect medications brought into the ED
Calcium channel blockers can cause many types of rhythms that can range from sinus bradycardia to complete heart block

Hyperglycemia is the result of the blocking of L-type calcium channels in the pancreas. This can help differentiate these ingestions from beta-blockers. There may also be a relationship between the severity of the ingestion and the extent of the hyperglycemia

Atropine may have little or no effect (likely to be more helpful in mild overdoses) a. Hypotension and bradycardia may be mutually exclusive, and the blood pressure may not respond to correction of bradycardia
Close monitoring of EKG changes and dysrhythmias

sections.title: Key Documentation
points: ' Repeat evaluation and documentation of signs and symptoms as patient clinical conditions may deteriorate rapidly
' Identification of possible etiology of poisoning
' Time of symptoms onset and time of initiation of exposure-specific treatments
' Therapy and response to therapy

EMR: Scene safety, primary survey, CPR, AED, BVM, bleeding control, oral airway, O2 therapy, epinephrine auto-injector for anaphylaxis, naloxone unit-dose auto-injector
EMT: EMR scope PLUS: NPA, supraglottic airway, CPAP, pulse oximetry, 12-lead ECG acquisition, oral glucose, aspirin, patient own nitroglycerin, albuterol SVN, IM epinephrine, blood glucose monitoring, cervical collar, traction splint
AEMT: EMT scope PLUS: waveform capnography, IO access, IV access initiation, IV/IO fluids, IV medications (dextrose, naloxone, epinephrine, glucagon, analgesics, antiemetics), subcutaneous/intranasal medications, nitroglycerin from stock
Paramedic: AEMT scope PLUS: ET intubation, RSI, cricothyrotomy, needle decompression, manual defibrillation, cardioversion, transcutaneous pacing, 12-lead ECG interpretation, all antidysrhythmics, vasopressors, all medication routes and classes

deterioration_flags: Contact medical direction for clinical deterioration or interventions outside standard scope
scope_notes: EMR scope is the minimum national standard — state/MCA may expand
All interventions subject to local medical director credentialing
Pediatric patients: use weight-based dosing and age-appropriate equipment
Contact medical direction when required by local protocol or for interventions outside standard scope
